author: Daniel Andersen
name: NativeBonsaiBinary

1337.note: SEED was not set in the recorded command shape, so the trainer default seed is 1337.

technique_summary: 5-layer 1024-dim SP8192 transformer with grouped binary STE linears, group size 128, unquantized embeddings/control tensors, split Muon optimizer, no TTT, and native binary packet-size accounting. Called Bonsai because it adopts the 1-bit structural idea from Bonsai's Qwen3 1-bit models. Final candidate includes DDP rank data offset and synchronized wallclock stop fixes.
non_record_reason: Only 4xH100s were available for this run, so it is not a valid 8xH100 leaderboard record attempt. The current result is also not competitive with SOTA record-track submissions.

base.notes: NativeBonsaiBinary follow-up run using the transfer_4xh100_5x1024 handoff bundle.

compliance.notes: Non-record 4xH100 run. LZMA packet plus code is 15,087,895 bytes; actual train stop was step 9824 at 587.561s. Evaluation timing should be filled from the imported score log.

fixes.ddp_rank_data_offset: Offsets TokenStream.file_idx by RANK % len(files) so DDP ranks train on distinct shards instead of duplicated minibatches.
fixes.synchronized_wallclock_stop: All-reduces a stop_now flag so all DDP ranks exit the training loop on the same step and avoid a save-time hang.
